definition.name: face_commutant_correspondence
definition.definition: (Alfsen-Shultz). Let A be a unital *-algebra and φ ∈ S(A) with GNS triple (π_φ, H_φ, ξ_φ). The face-commutant correspondence is the affine isomorphism F_{S(A)}(φ) ≅ {T ∈ π_φ(A)'^+ : ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1} given by the map T ↦ ω_T where ω_T(a) = ⟨π_φ(a)Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩. The inverse map sends ψ ∈ F_{S(A)}(φ) (with ψ ≤ λφ for some λ > 0) to the unique T ∈ π_φ(A)' with 0 ≤ T ≤ λI satisfying ψ(a) = ⟨π_φ(a)Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ (the Radon-Nikodym type theorem). The dimensional consequence is dim(F_{S(A)}(φ)) = dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) - 1, where the -1 comes from the normalisation ⟨Tξ_φ, ξ_φ⟩ = 1 imposing one real constraint on the self-adjoint part of the commutant. For C*-algebras, the face is affinely isomorphic to the normal state space of the von Neumann algebra π_φ(A)''. References: Alfsen-Shultz, State Spaces of Operator Algebras (2001), Thm 5.34; Geometry of State Spaces (2003), §8.
